title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Poirot confronts Valerie Hobhouse regarding a series of thefts involving Miss Silvia. He deduces that Valerie orchestrated the incidents—which included Silvia stealing items and tearing a silk scarf—to help Silvia gain the attention of Colin McNab. Valerie admits to this, explaining her motive was a combination of "stupid goodwill" toward Silvia and a desire to tease the conceited Colin. Valerie further confesses that when Silvia stole a genuine ring belonging to Patricia Lean, Valerie took it to prevent trouble. Driven by a gambling addiction and debts from a club in Mayfair, Valerie admits she replaced the diamond in Patricia's ring with a zirconia and sold the original diamond. Poirot advises Valerie to confess her actions to Patricia and return the diamond.

The conversation is interrupted by Mrs. Haberdash, who announces that Mrs. Nicoletti has been found dead on the street. Although Mrs. Nicoletti was previously thought to be a drunk, she is deceased, and an autopsy is scheduled for Tuesday. Mrs. Haberdash notes that Mrs. Nicoletti had recently become unpredictable and expressed feelings of being unsafe.

Later, at Scotland Yard, Poirot meets with Inspectors Wilding, Bell, and Sharpe. Inspector Japp joins the meeting to discuss the broader investigation. Japp reveals that there has been a significant influx of narcotics, primarily heroin and some cocaine, into the country over the last eighteen months, managed by smuggling rings with bases across Europe.
